paper_title: Opportunity analysis of agriculture products in Sri Lanka: Measuring markets and feasibility
research_answer: Global trade in selected agricultural products is highly concentrated. Just five market blocs — the EU, USA, Middle East, East Asia, and India — account for 80.7% of total world trade in these products. The EU alone commands 42.0% of world imports (about $40.6 billion), making it by far the dominant market. The USA is the second-largest at 18.8% ($18.2 billion), followed by East Asia (China, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, Hong Kong) at 15.4% ($14.9 billion). This means that nearly any agricultural export strategy targeting these sectors must prioritize the EU and USA above all others.
supporting_quotes: EU Market 40.6 [USD, bn], 42.0% [of world]; USA Market 18.2 [USD, bn], 18.8% [of world]
East Asian Market (China, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, Hong Kong) 14.9 [USD, bn], 15.4% [of world]
